title: psychotria pendula subsp. genuina
intro: Psychotria pendula subsp. genuina, also known as the 'hanging gold' or 'gold necklace vine', is a tropical plant species belonging to the Rubiaceae family. The leaves and stems of this plant have been traditionally used in herbal medicine to treat various health conditions, including fever, rheumatism, and skin problems, due to its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The medicinal properties of Psychotria pendula subsp. genuina have been attributed to its high content of flavonoids, phenolic acids, and other bioactive compounds, which contribute to its health benefits such as antioxidant, anti-inflammatory, and antiseptic activities. Herbal preparations of this plant include teas, infusions, and decoctions, which can be used to promote wound healing, reduce pain and inflammation, and support overall well-being.
